combine.title: Combine PDF files
combine.intro: Add two or more PDF files to merge them into a single document.
combine.reorderHint: Drag to reorder. The combined document follows this order.
combine.empty: No files yet. Add at least two to combine.
combine.needTwo: Add at least two files to combine.
combine.noFileSelected: No file selected yet — file picking arrives in a later phase.
combine.perRowNote: Per-row page-range editing arrives in Phase 2.

confirmClose.title: Unsaved changes
confirmClose.body: This document has unsaved changes. Save before closing?

exportEngine.title: Export to PDF
exportEngine.note: The hybrid engine selector picks pdf-lib by default and falls back to Chromium for encrypted or structurally-flagged PDFs. Phase 2 wires the actual export pipeline; this dialog ships now so the UX is consistent.

export.title: Export to Office
export.fmtWordDesc: Editable text document with paragraphs, headings, tables.
export.fmtExcelDesc: Workbook of tables detected via line-grid analysis; best for tabular PDFs.
export.fmtPptxDesc: One slide per page; positioned text and embedded images.
export.fmtJpegDesc: Lossy raster image; quality slider in next step.

ocrRun.title: Run OCR
ocrRun.invalidateTitle: Re-running OCR will replace existing results
ocrRun.invalidateBody: OCR results for this document already exist. Running again will replace them. Existing signatures may be invalidated if the text layer changes.
ocrRun.notPublicationQuality: OCR text is best-effort recognition, not publication-quality transcription. Always verify against the original.
ocrRun.honestyReminderAccuracy: OCR accuracy depends on scan quality; low-confidence words may be incorrect and will be highlighted in orange. OCR runs locally — no cloud upload; language packs are downloaded from Tesseract's official upstream on first use.
ocrRun.honestyReminderDuplicate: Re-running OCR adds another text layer. If this page already contains OCR text from a prior pass, multiple OCR passes may produce duplicate selectable text. Undo the previous pass first if you want clean output.
ocrRun.invalidateHeading: This PDF has a cryptographic signature
ocrRun.invalidateBody1: Running OCR modifies the document's page content, which will invalidate any prior PAdES signature. The signature audit log will record this invalidation; the signed file on disk is unchanged until you Save.
ocrRun.invalidateBody2: If you proceed, the original signature is no longer verifiable. You can re-sign the document after OCR.
ocrRun.dontAskAgain: Don't ask me again for this session
ocrRun.confirmCancel: Cancel OCR? Partial output will be discarded.
ocrRun.cancelledBody: The OCR run was cancelled. No changes were applied to the document.
ocrRun.summaryNotLoaded: OCR finished but the summary is not yet loaded.
ocrRun.doneReminderBody: OCR-extracted text becomes part of the document and is committed to disk on Save. Low-confidence words may be incorrect; review them before relying on the extracted text for legal or regulatory use.

padesSign.title: Sign with PFX certificate
padesSign.notLegalAdvice: A digital signature proves the file was not altered after signing; it is not legal advice and does not by itself make a document legally binding.
padesSign.passwordNeverSaved: Your password is never logged or saved. It's discarded immediately after the cert is loaded.
padesSign.passwordDiscarded: Your password is never logged or saved. It was discarded immediately after the certificate was loaded.
padesSign.errPfxDecode: PFX could not be decoded. Is the file valid?
padesSign.clickSignInfo: Click Sign to apply the signature. This may take up to 30 seconds if timestamping is enabled.

signatureCapture.title: Add visual signature
signatureCapture.drawnCanvasLabel: Drawing signature with mouse or pen — use the Image tab to upload instead
signatureCapture.dragDropImage: Drag-drop a PNG or JPEG into this area to import.
signatureCapture.errUnsupportedImage: Only PNG or JPEG images are supported.
signatureCapture.errImageDecode: The image could not be decoded.

languagePack.title: Language Packs
languagePack.offlineNote: Language packs are downloaded once and used offline thereafter.
languagePack.honestyReminder: Language packs download from Tesseract's official upstream (tessdata.projectnaptha.com). Every download is SHA-256-verified against the shipped catalog. OCR itself runs locally — no PDF bytes ever leave this machine.

scan.title: Scan from device
scan.deferred: Scanner integration arrives in Phase 5.1. For now, scan with your operating system's scan utility, then open or drag the saved PDF here to run OCR.
scan.deferredBody: Phase 5 ships the file-import + OCR path only. Native scanner (WIA/TWAIN) bindings are deferred to Phase 5.1 — no MIT-compatible Node WIA binding survives the project's open-source maturity bar at this time.
scan.saveThenDrag: Save the scan as PDF, then drag it into this app. You can then run OCR via Tools → Run OCR.

imageImport.title: Insert Image
imageImport.modeOverlay: As an overlay on the current page
imageImport.errUnsupported: Only PNG, JPEG, and TIFF files are supported.
imageImport.multiPageTiffWarning: Multi-page TIFF: only the first page will be used.
imageImport.userSpaceNote: Position is in PDF user-space (origin bottom-left). After insert, you can click-drag the overlay handles on the canvas to refine placement.

mailMerge.title: Mail Merge
mailMerge.flattenForms: Flatten forms in output (irreversible interactivity loss)

help.title: PDF Viewer & Editor — Help
help.shortcutsLead: yes* = enabled in Phase 1 but with the Save fidelity caveat noted below.
help.limSaveFidelityBody: Save produces a valid PDF, but Phase 1 does not yet preserve your edits — Phase 2 ships the edit-replay engine. Rotations, deletions, reorders, inserted blanks, and annotations are not written into the saved bytes.
help.limPrintExportBody: are disabled (Ctrl+P, Ctrl+Shift+P). The hybrid pdf-lib + Chromium engine ships in Phase 2.
help.limUndoRedoBody: are disabled in Phase 1 (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y / Ctrl+F). Phase 2 wires the command stack and the in-document text search.
help.limBookmarksBody: are read-only / not yet wired. Phase 2 adds creation, nesting, and image embedding.
help.roadmapPhase2Body: edit-replay engine, Print / Export to PDF, Find, Undo/Redo, bookmarks authoring, image import, replace-only text editing.
help.roadmapPhase6Body: Export to Word / Excel / PowerPoint / image.
help.escToClose: Press Esc or click outside this dialog to close.

about.title: About PDF_Viewer_Editor
about.tagline: An original, permissively-licensed desktop PDF viewer and editor.
about.installConfirmBody: You have unsaved changes. Installing the update will restart the app. Save your changes first, or discard them and install now.
about.statusUpToDate: You are running the latest available version.
about.statusNotConfigured: Update channel not configured (placeholder). Updates check GitHub releases, but the release channel is a placeholder until the project is published — updates will not download until it is configured. The update client itself is wired and ready.
about.licenseBody: PDF_Viewer_Editor is open-source under the MIT license.
about.builtWith: Built with: pdf.js (Apache-2.0), pdf-lib (MIT), tesseract.js (Apache-2.0), docx / exceljs / pptxgenjs (MIT), i18next / react-i18next / electron-updater (MIT), node-signpdf (MIT), better-sqlite3 (MIT), and others.

telemetryDebug.title: Collected usage data (this session)
telemetryDebug.bufferedSummary_one: {{count}} event buffered (max {{max}}). Nothing has left your computer — these counts live only in memory.
telemetryDebug.bufferedSummary_other: {{count}} events buffered (max {{max}}). Nothing has left your computer — these counts live only in memory.
